purpose: Aligns items along the main axis (horizontal by default).
syntax: justify-content: [flex-start | flex-end | center | space-between | space-around | space-evenly];
proTip: Use 'space-between' for navbars where logos are left and links are right.
commonMistake: Applying justify-content to a block element that isn't display: flex.

purpose: Aligns items along the cross axis (vertical by default).
proTip: Use 'stretch' (default) to make all children fill the height of the container.

purpose: Defines the column structure of a grid container.
example: grid-template-columns: 1fr 2fr 1fr; // Three columns, middle is double width
proTip: Use 'repeat(auto-fit, minmax(200px, 1fr))' for a perfectly responsive grid without media queries.
commonMistake: Mixing grid and flexbox on the same element without understanding the child impact.

purpose: Sets the spacing between rows and columns.
proTip: Gap is now supported in Flexbox too in modern browsers!
commonMistake: Using margin on children to create gaps, which messes up the outer container alignment.

purpose: Positions an element relative to its nearest positioned ancestor.
example: position: absolute; top: 10px; right: 10px; // Fixed to top-right of parent
proTip: Ensure the parent has 'position: relative' otherwise the element will float relative to the body.
commonMistake: Forgetting to set a parent relative, causing the absolute element to 'fly' across the page.

purpose: Toggles between relative and fixed positioning based on scroll position.
example: position: sticky; top: 0; // Stays at top while scrolling the section
proTip: Sticky doesn't work if any ancestor has 'overflow: hidden'.
commonMistake: Not specifying a threshold (like top: 0), which makes the sticky behavior fail.

purpose: Includes padding and border in the element's total width and height.
example: div { box-sizing: border-box; width: 100px; padding: 20px; } // Total width remains 100px
proTip: Apply this to all elements using the universal selector `* { box-sizing: border-box; }` at the start of every project.
commonMistake: Using content-box (default), which makes a '100% width' element overflow its parent if padding is added.

purpose: Stores reusable values for consistent styling.
proTip: Use variables for themes (Light/Dark mode) by simply changing the variable values in the :root.
commonMistake: Defining variables in a specific class and trying to access them in a sibling element.
